astrocade-qa-calibration-tool.title: Astrocade AI QA Calibration Tool
astrocade-qa-calibration-tool.description: Designed and operated a human-in-the-loop calibration system for Astrocade's AI-powered UGC review pipeline. It samples completed QA reviews, records explicit ground truth and role-scoped diagnostics, and compares QA, final review, and creator-feedback quality through immutable calibration signals.

details.proofRole: human-in-the-loop AI systems for creator-facing moderation

situation.title: Overview
situation.description: Astrocade needed fast publishing decisions for an AI-powered UGC platform without treating QA or auto-review as final authority. Quality decisions needed a repeatable handoff from sampled QA evidence to final review and creator-facing feedback.

situation.title: Quality risk
situation.description: False, repeated, or inconsistent rejections created friction for creators and slowed publishing. Disagreement between automated decisions and reviewers had to become visible, explainable, and actionable.

situation.title: Operating loop
situation.description: The workflow connects sampled game context, QA outcomes, explicit ground truth, calibrated failure modes, final-review comparison, and creator-feedback quality. Human judgment is an accountable control point, not a late-stage exception.

task.title: Design the operating model
task.description: Create a calibration workflow that re-QAs completed game samples and turns QA, final review, and creator feedback into one continuous, evidence-based decision loop.

task.title: Make quality measurable
task.description: Define explicit ground truth, QA and final-review correctness, failure modes, agreement, and feedback actionability so quality is based on observable signals rather than reviewer sentiment.

task.title: Protect creator outcomes
task.description: Establish structured feedback and operational guardrails for review that preserve safety while testing whether a creator can act on the decision without guessing or re-discovering the issue.

actions.title: Built role-scoped calibration sessions
actions.description: Implemented repeatable sessions that load a completed game sample beside QA and final-review context, then collect structured calibration signals without free-text scoring.

actions.title: Established explicit ground truth
actions.description: Separated the expected platform decision from the QA decision, allowing the system to compute QA correctness and then diagnose why an incorrect decision occurred.

actions.title: Defined QA and final-review guardrails
actions.description: Added constrained failure-mode taxonomies for QA and final review so disagreement, policy misapplication, insufficient review, and tooling errors can be analyzed separately.

actions.title: Closed creator feedback loops
actions.description: Scored whether feedback is actionable and checks for a reproducible creator-facing formula, then saves immutable calibration records that can inform documentation and workflow updates.

results.title: A measurable quality-control loop
results.description: Made QA incorrect decisions, false rejections, missed blocking issues, approval precision and recall, final-review correctness, QA-final agreement, and feedback actionability visible with counts and legacy-data exclusions.

results.title: More consistent decisions
results.description: Ground truth, structured failure modes, and separate QA and final-review comparisons create a shared basis for calibration, policy discussion, and reviewer guidance.

results.title: Creator feedback becomes a quality signal
results.description: Actionability checks turn creator feedback into a concrete review-quality measure, so improvements can target decisions that are safe, clear, and usable.

wizzo.title: Wizzo
wizzo.description: Designed and built an AI mentor product system that turns chat, connected work context, goals, and follow-up into actionable quests.

details.proofRole: AI product systems connecting intent, work context, and accountable follow-through

situation.title: Intent-to-Action Gap
situation.description: AI conversations often stop at advice. Wizzo reframes that moment as an execution problem: how to turn goals, blockers, deadlines, and recent progress into next steps a user can actually finish.

situation.title: Product Context
situation.description: The public beta positions Wizzo as an AI adventure mentor that combines chat, voice, connected work context, achievement loops, and quest-based follow-through.

situation.title: System Context
situation.description: The product needed to connect AI guidance with real work surfaces while keeping privacy, account controls, and product trust visible from the start.

task.title: Design an AI Mentor Loop
task.description: Create a product loop where chat can capture intent, remember execution context, suggest grounded next steps, and translate progress into quests.

task.title: Connect Work Context
task.description: Support Drive, Gmail, Calendar, and technical workflows so the AI mentor can reason from the artifacts and deadlines that shape real follow-through.

task.title: Make AI Actions Grounded
task.description: Pair conversation with tools for search, research, code execution, image generation, files, and voice so the assistant can move beyond generic coaching.

task.title: Preserve User Control
task.description: Build account export, confirmed deletion, and privacy-aware controls into the product system so personal progress data remains manageable.

actions.title: Product System Architecture
actions.description: Designed the core experience around main chat, execution-aware memory, quest tracking, achievements, and connector-backed work context.

actions.title: Full-Stack Implementation
actions.description: Built the product with Next.js, TypeScript, Postgres, Drizzle ORM, Neon, Vercel, OpenAI integrations, and Google workspace connectors.

actions.title: AI Workflow Surfaces
actions.description: Created mentor interactions for goal planning, progress reflection, grounded research, connected notes, and voice-supported follow-up.

actions.title: Trust and Account Controls
actions.description: Added export and confirmed deletion paths covering product, AI, notification, integration, ML-derived, and community records.

results.title: Public Beta
results.description: Shipped a public beta with a marketing site, live web app, AI mentor interface, quest system, and connected work context.

results.title: AI Product Proof
results.description: Demonstrated an AI-native product system that combines chat, memory, work connectors, tools, privacy controls, and habit-forming progress loops.

results.title: Portfolio Relevance
results.description: Serves as proof of product design, engineering execution, AI workflow design, and operational thinking across a real shipped SaaS surface.

x-games.title: X Games
x-games.description: Built and operate an AI-first social game platform that turns X posts into playable browser games, then supports discovery, tuning, replay, sharing, reporting, and ranked competition around the generated catalog.

details.proofRole: Direct product proof for AI-native generation, social distribution, game tuning, and ranked play in a live browser platform.

situation.title: From Post to Playable System
situation.description: X Games is a live AI-native product loop: start from a post on X, generate a playable browser game with Grok, then make that game discoverable, replayable, tunable, shareable, and rankable.

situation.title: Product Challenge
situation.description: The experience had to make a probabilistic generation process feel immediate and legible while preserving the source post, game premise, creator identity, and a clear path into play.

situation.title: Operating Context
situation.description: The system connects a live X feed, AI-assisted concept generation, generated game art and mechanics, browser gameplay, social distribution, moderation, and leaderboard behavior.

task.title: Design the Generation Loop
task.description: Create a coherent path from source post to game concept, generated assets, playable runtime, and persistent game page without hiding where the game came from.

task.title: Create Player Control
task.description: Give players bounded ways to tune pace, difficulty, run length, hazards, power-ups, feedback, mechanics, and replay focus without losing the original game premise.

task.title: Build a Social Game Ecosystem
task.description: Turn generated games into persistent social objects with creator attribution, discovery, saves, reporting, engagement signals, leaderboards, and replay paths.

actions.title: Connected Source Context to Generation
actions.description: Built feed and game-detail surfaces that keep the originating account and post visible while carrying the generated title, genre, premise, art, and playable runtime into the product.

actions.title: Designed the Generated-Game Cockpit
actions.description: Combined game playback, bounded tuning controls, play statistics, library behavior, reporting, and source-post access in a single product surface.

actions.title: Built Discovery and Competition
actions.description: Created trending discovery and all-time ranking surfaces that organize generated games by creator, genre, play activity, and direct replay actions.

results.title: Live AI Product System
results.description: The result is an operating browser platform rather than a static generation demo: real posts become persistent game pages inside a discoverable catalog.

results.title: Human Control After Generation
results.description: Players can inspect source context, tune generated behavior, save or report a game, replay it, and compare its activity through product-level controls.

results.title: Persistent Social Proof
results.description: Trending and leaderboard surfaces make generated games browseable and comparable, connecting AI creation to creator identity, distribution, engagement, and ranked play.

gaia.title: Gaia
gaia.description: Turned Starbucks store data into spatial operations and training workflows, connecting analytics, prototyping, and enterprise internal-tool UX.

details.proofRole: Direct evidence of leading UX design and Unity prototyping for enterprise spatial operations, from opportunity framing through physical-device and immersive test states.

situation.title: Data Needed Spatial Context
situation.description: Gaia explored how Starbucks initiative and store data could become contextual information for collaboration, planning, training, and operational review rather than remain isolated in flat files and dashboards.

situation.title: Two Connected Surfaces
situation.description: The concept joined Gaia VisiPad for browsing initiative files and configuration with Gaia VR for inspecting store and portfolio context in an immersive environment.

situation.title: Enterprise Design Constraint
situation.description: The interface had to preserve recognizable project metadata and controls while making a new spatial-computing workflow understandable to partners reviewing unfamiliar 3D environments.

task.title: Lead the Experience Design
task.description: Own the UX and interface direction for the tablet and immersive workflows, translating the opportunity into user stories, information architecture, and reviewable prototypes.

task.title: Build the Spatial Prototype
task.description: Develop Unity scenes that connect store selection, project context, role controls, and spatial models without losing the operational information behind them.

task.title: Test the Interaction Model
task.description: Use physical-device and headset states to evaluate whether the workflow remained legible as it moved from a conventional interface into a 3D environment.

actions.title: Framed the Opportunity
actions.description: Translated the project brief into how-might-we questions, initial use cases, and user stories covering collaboration, store data, initiative files, and spatial decision support.

actions.title: Designed the VisiPad Workflow
actions.description: Created Figma flows for initiative-file browsing, project metadata, upload and deletion states, and store configuration before validating the interface on tablet hardware.

actions.title: Built the Unity Environments
actions.description: Moved from 3D wireframes into Unity store-selection and store-overview scenes with spatial models, store cards, role controls, and navigable operational context.

actions.title: Tested Across Modalities
actions.description: Reviewed the experience on a physical tablet and in an immersive headset state, using the resulting evidence to connect conventional controls with the spatial workflow.

results.title: End-to-End Spatial Prototype
results.description: The project produced a reviewable path from initiative-file management on a tablet to store selection and store inspection inside a Unity environment.

results.title: Operational Context in 3D
results.description: Store cards, project metadata, role controls, and physical layouts remain visible across the prototype, demonstrating how enterprise information can accompany spatial navigation.

results.title: Traceable Design Progression
results.description: The evidence set connects opportunity framing, user stories, 2D flows, 3D wireframes, physical-device review, immersive testing, and final spatial scenes without relying on unsupported impact claims.

apt-plus.title: APT+
apt-plus.description: Led design and engineering for Ford APT+, a production time-study system that reduced manual capture and validation work, centralized operational data, and documented approximately $1M in annual savings per plant.

details.proofRole: Direct production evidence of leading operational UX and engineering strategy for manufacturing time studies, with documented workflow and financial outcomes.

situation.title: Time Studies Drove Production Decisions
situation.description: Ford manufacturing teams relied on time studies to identify bottlenecks and improve production lines, making capture accuracy and review speed operationally consequential.

situation.title: Manual Capture Created Friction
situation.description: The documented process was labor-intensive and error-prone, while collecting, validating, and interpreting study data took too long.

situation.title: Disconnected Data Limited Insight
situation.description: Results lived across disconnected processes, making it difficult to compare studies, expose work balance, and turn collected observations into repeatable decisions.

task.title: Improve Capture Accuracy
task.description: Create a field-facing workflow that reduced manual entry and made operation timing, task categories, and validation states explicit.

task.title: Centralize Review Context
task.description: Bring operation rows, time values, work-balance views, and editable process context into one system instead of another isolated capture tool.

task.title: Support Production-Scale Decisions
task.description: Design the product for cross-functional use and repeatable analysis across plants, with evidence that operators and decision-makers could inspect.

actions.title: Led Field Research
actions.description: Investigated the existing time-study process with project managers, software engineers, researchers, and operational stakeholders to isolate the most costly and error-prone steps.

actions.title: Prototyped the Capture Model
actions.description: Mapped task selection, timing events, work categories, and submission behavior in annotated prototypes, then refined the system from stakeholder feedback.

actions.title: Built the Production Workflow
actions.description: Combined operation data, timing values, work-balance bars, task editing, validation controls, and automated analysis in a unified production interface.

actions.title: Connected Movement to Analysis
actions.description: Extended the review model with walk-pattern analysis so workstation geometry and operator movement could be evaluated alongside captured time-study data.

results.title: Approximately $1M Annual Savings per Plant
results.description: The project record attributes the savings to reduced manual errors and faster time-study execution at each facility.

results.title: Faster Capture and Validation
results.description: Automated workflows reduced the time spent collecting and validating study data while preserving detailed operation-level evidence.

results.title: Centralized Operational Insight
results.description: The production system gave decision-makers a shared surface for performance metrics, work balance, movement analysis, and process-improvement review across plants.

transcribe.title: Transcribe
transcribe.description: Designed and built a React speech-to-text workflow for Starbucks drive-throughs, combining partner-facing transcription, accessibility research, interface prototyping, and in-store evaluation.

details.proofRole: Direct implementation evidence for accessible voice interaction in a high-traffic retail workflow, from research and interface design through React development and in-store testing.

situation.title: Noise Interrupted Drive-Through Communication
situation.description: The project brief identifies noisy store environments, acoustic accessibility barriers, and high demand from retail partners as recurring causes of communication friction.

situation.title: Partners and Customers Shared the Workflow
situation.description: A customer order had to become readable speech-to-text at the point of sale while preserving a fast partner-facing interaction inside an existing drive-through process.

situation.title: The Pilot Had Physical Constraints
situation.description: The concept paired a customer microphone, partner touchscreen, Dell display, and existing point-of-sale station, so interface decisions had to work within a real counter setup.

task.title: Make Speech Visible in Real Time
task.description: Create a transcription experience that let partners confirm customer orders without relying only on noisy audio or manual note-taking.

task.title: Design for Accessible Control
task.description: Make listening, muted, correction, confirmation, and order states legible for people with different communication needs.

task.title: Validate the Retail Workflow
task.description: Move beyond a standalone interface by testing the product in the store environment and evaluating how it fit the partner and customer interaction.

actions.title: Researched Accessibility Needs
actions.description: Studied partner and customer communication needs with explicit attention to people with disabilities and the constraints of high-traffic retail environments.

actions.title: Designed MVP and Stretch Flows
actions.description: Created Figma flows for ordering, transcription, microphone control, confirmation, and completion states across a bounded MVP and expanded interaction model.

actions.title: Built the Transcription Application
actions.description: Developed the interface in React and integrated Google Cloud Speech-to-Text so spoken customer orders could appear on the partner-facing display in real time.

actions.title: Tested Interface and Store Context
actions.description: Reviewed muted and listening states, conducted usability testing with people with disabilities, and evaluated the prototype inside a physical Starbucks counter workflow.

results.title: 30% Transcription-Accuracy Improvement
results.description: The case-study record reports the measured improvement after the speech-to-text workflow was refined and evaluated.

results.title: 50% Less Manual Note-Taking Time
results.description: The documented outcome connects real-time transcription with reduced dependence on manual note capture during the retail interaction.

results.title: In-Context Pilot Evidence
results.description: The evidence set shows the partner display operating beside the point-of-sale system and staff reviewing the experience at the store counter.

geovoice.title: GeoVoice
geovoice.description: Designed a map-based stakeholder-feedback system that connects project layers, location-specific comments, participant context, and public-facing infrastructure review in one workflow.

details.proofRole: Direct design and prototype evidence for a geospatial stakeholder-feedback workflow, from location selection and structured comments through map layers and regional project applications.

situation.title: Public Input Was Disconnected from Place
situation.description: Infrastructure and environmental projects needed feedback from community members, agencies, and private partners, but forums and paper surveys separated comments from the exact locations under discussion.

situation.title: GIS Context Was Hard to Share
situation.description: Project teams had rich spatial data, but conventional GIS tools were difficult for non-technical participants to navigate and did not provide a common public-feedback workflow.

situation.title: The Evidence Had to Stay Traceable
situation.description: A useful response needed to preserve who was commenting, what type of issue they identified, and where that input belonged on the project map.

task.title: Connect Feedback to a Location
task.description: Create a simple path for selecting an address, parcel, current location, or map point before submitting project feedback.

task.title: Make Project Layers Inspectable
task.description: Keep routes, structures, notifications, land-use data, and basemap controls visible so each comment could be reviewed in geographic context.

task.title: Support Different Public Projects
task.description: Design the interaction model to work across transmission, offshore-wind, municipal, and environmental-planning contexts without changing its core feedback loop.

actions.title: Mapped Stakeholder and GIS Constraints
actions.description: The project record documents sessions with planners, environmental experts, and community members alongside an evaluation of existing GIS and collaboration tools.

actions.title: Prototyped Location and Comment Flows
actions.description: Designed map layouts, layered-data displays, location-selection states, and a structured submission form, then refined the workflow through the iterative testing documented in the case-study record.

actions.title: Integrated Maps, Layers, and Participation
actions.description: The resulting interface combines route and project layers, basemap and measurement controls, account prompts, categorized comments, and geospatial review within the same public-facing surface.

results.title: One Inspectable Feedback Loop
results.description: The reviewed artifacts show location selection, comment capture, map layers, project notifications, and public review operating as one connected workflow.

results.title: Faster Planning Feedback
results.description: The case-study record reports that real-time updates and direct comment submission cut weeks from traditional planning cycles; this outcome is retained as an attributed project claim.

results.title: Reusable Across Infrastructure Contexts
results.description: The evidence set includes both a transmission-line project and the Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind deployment, demonstrating reuse of the map, layer, notification, and comment model across regions.

speakeasy.title: SpeakEasy
speakeasy.description: Designed and built a Quest 3 voice-first accessibility prototype in Unity, using Meta Voice SDK, multimodal feedback, and three research-through-design iterations to reduce dependence on handheld XR controllers.

details.client: Master of Design in Experience Design, SJSU
details.proofRole: Direct research and implementation evidence for a voice-first mixed-reality accessibility system, spanning participatory inquiry, Unity and Quest development, Meta Voice SDK integration, measured prototype iteration, and public thesis defense.

situation.title: Controllers Created an Entry Barrier
situation.description: The thesis focuses on people with limited grip strength, upper-limb mobility challenges, and low muscle tone who can be excluded when XR requires two handheld controllers and precise physical input.

situation.title: Voice Needed to Provide Agency
situation.description: Research participants described fatigue, frustration, and difficulty with controller-based interaction, making hands-free session control a functional accessibility requirement rather than a convenience layer.

situation.title: Recognition Failure Could Break Trust
situation.description: Early trials exposed false triggers, response delay, command recall, and accent support as barriers, so listening state, confirmation, error recovery, and fallback guidance had to remain visible.

task.title: Make Voice the Primary Control Channel
task.description: Support session start, progression, completion, breathing, visualization, lighting, and affirmation interactions without requiring a handheld controller.

task.title: Make System State Perceivable
task.description: Pair spoken feedback with gaze-anchored text, color, sound, and confidence cues so users could tell when the system was listening, processing, succeeding, or asking for clarification.

task.title: Measure Accessibility Through Iteration
task.description: Use prototype metrics and participant sessions to evaluate task completion, usability, latency, comfort, recognition limits, and the ten-pillar interaction framework.

task.title: Keep Incomplete Work Explicit
task.description: Separate implemented features from partial and planned pillars, especially paraphrase coverage, pacing, redirection, privacy controls, and broader language support.

actions.title: Grounded the System in Participatory Research
actions.description: Combined literature review, expert input, a 42-person survey, participatory co-design, and accessibility synthesis to translate lived barriers into hands-free agency, transparent feedback, and flexible pacing requirements.

actions.title: Iterated Across Three Prototype Generations
actions.description: Moved from a Wizard-of-Oz paper and Figma test to a live Quest 3 alpha in Unity, then to a beta with faster response, confidence cues, adaptive lighting, and positive affirmation modules.

actions.title: Implemented the Quest 3 Voice System
actions.description: Built the Unity 2022.3 application with Meta Voice SDK and Wit.ai, wake-word activation, command variants, intent and confidence handling, TTS, audio and visual feedback, world-space UI, and voice-controlled therapy modules.

actions.title: Tested with Eight Final Participants
actions.description: Ran structured Quest 3 sessions across varied ages, XR familiarity, ability, and professional backgrounds, measuring SUS, voice-only task completion, response latency, error recovery, comfort, and feedback clarity.

results.title: Measured Alpha-to-Beta Improvement
results.description: The process-book record reports latency improving from 530 ms to 380 ms, voice-only task completion increasing from 64% to 79%, and SUS increasing from 71 to 79 between the alpha and beta prototypes.

results.title: Six Pillars Implemented
results.description: The final framework records six of ten accessibility pillars as implemented, two as partial, and two as planned; this status is preserved instead of presenting the system as universally complete.

results.title: Known Recognition Limits
results.description: Participant sessions identified English-only recognition, accent sensitivity, command recall, and confidence-cue clarity as remaining constraints, providing a concrete research agenda rather than an unsupported scalability claim.

exhibition.title: Public Thesis Exhibition
exhibition.description: Delivered a gallery installation with a Quest headset, live Unity experience, project framing, and process evidence for hands-on public review.

exhibition.title: Thesis Defense and Process Book
exhibition.description: Presented the research, prototype progression, participant findings, ten-pillar framework, and limitations in a public M.Des defense and a 41-page process book.

exhibition.title: Defined Follow-Up Work
exhibition.description: Broader accent and language support, clearer redirection, privacy controls, on-device storage choices, and deeper personalization remain explicit future work.

petition-ready.title: PetitionReady
petition-ready.description: Built an AI-assisted bankruptcy intake readiness console for paralegals, focused on case blockers, document gaps, readiness scoring, client follow-up, and attorney-review packet preparation without crossing into legal advice.

details.proofRole: Direct product and implementation evidence for an AI-assisted legal-operations workflow, spanning typed bankruptcy intake models, deterministic readiness analysis, server-side structured model outputs, fallback behavior, and explicit paralegal-to-attorney review boundaries.

situation.title: Exceptions surfaced too late
situation.description: Bankruptcy firms lose time when intake packets are incomplete, income data conflicts, creditor addresses are missing, documents are rejected, or payment status is unclear. Those exceptions often surface late, after a paralegal has already started assembling a packet for attorney review.

situation.title: The handoff lacked an operating view
situation.description: The product needed to answer one practical workflow question: can this client matter move toward attorney review, and if not, what exact blocker should the paralegal resolve next?

situation.title: Legal scope required a hard boundary
situation.description: The prototype intentionally focuses on readiness, blockers, evidence, and attorney-review preparation instead of automated filing, legal advice, eligibility decisions, or petition generation.

task.title: Make readiness inspectable
task.description: Design and build a working dashboard that gives paralegals a clear view of active matters, blocker counts, readiness scores, queue status, and next actions.

task.title: Ground AI in the current packet
task.description: Use structured AI outputs to summarize blockers, find conflicts, draft client follow-ups, and compile attorney-review briefs from the current case packet.

task.title: Preserve reviewer access without credentials
task.description: Make the demo useful without credentials through deterministic local fallback analysis, while preserving the same UI shape when model calls are unavailable.

actions.title: Modeled repeatable case states
actions.description: Created seeded Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 matters with client facts, document states, retainer status, issues, readiness stages, raw notes, and blocker metadata.

actions.title: Built the paralegal operating console
actions.description: Implemented readiness summary cards, matter queue pagination, status badges, selected matter details, top blockers, next paralegal action, and attorney-review item surfaces. Readiness starts at 100, subtracts documented issue weights, and maps each matter to attorney review, cleanup, or blocked state.

actions.title: Implemented structured server-side Copilot
actions.description: Built server-side Paralegal Copilot modes for blocker analysis, conflict checks, client follow-up drafts, and attorney-review brief compilation using the OpenAI Responses API, structured JSON output, and Zod validation.

actions.title: Kept deterministic fallback equivalent
actions.description: Created local analysis for readiness scoring, blocker detection, follow-up generation, and brief assembly so reviewers can evaluate the full workflow without an OpenAI key.

results.title: One blocker-to-review workflow
results.description: The prototype turns intake state into a repeatable queue, readiness score, issue list, evidence snapshot, next action, and attorney-review handoff instead of scattering those decisions across disconnected views.

results.title: Inspectable human-in-the-loop boundary
results.description: PetitionReady grounds Copilot outputs in supplied case evidence, keeps paralegal and attorney review visible, and explicitly avoids legal conclusions, filing decisions, and automated petition generation.

results.title: Production-minded prototype with explicit gaps
results.description: The public repo and Vercel demo include typed domain models, structured AI response validation, focused Vitest coverage, timeout and fallback behavior, and seeded local data. Authentication, persistence, audit logs, and production legal workflows remain deliberately out of scope.

creative-supply-engine.title: Creative Supply Engine
creative-supply-engine.description: Built a Python creative automation pipeline that turns structured campaign briefs and reusable product assets into localized AI-assisted social creatives across 1:1, 9:16, and 16:9 formats, with deterministic logo/text overlays, compliance checks, run logs, and review galleries.

details.proofRole: Direct implementation evidence for a reuse-first creative operations pipeline, spanning structured campaign briefs, optional OpenAI image generation, deterministic brand composition, multi-market localization, compliance checks, provenance logs, and human review packaging.

situation.title: Generation was only one step
situation.description: Campaign teams need to produce consistent, localized social creative across multiple formats without rebuilding every asset by hand.

situation.title: Brand rules needed deterministic control
situation.description: AI image generation can help create campaign hero assets, but brand, copy, compliance, sizing, and review requirements still need deterministic controls.

situation.title: A sample campaign defined the operating context
situation.description: The fictional Pulse Beverages campaign uses reusable product assets, a structured YAML brief, English and Spanish market copy, three social ratios, and required logo and prohibited-word checks.

task.title: Create a reuse-first pipeline
task.description: Create a CLI workflow that can read a campaign brief, reuse existing hero assets when available, generate missing assets when configured, and output production-ready social ratios.

task.title: Scale copy and composition across markets
task.description: Support multiple products and locales while preserving brand rules, CTA text, campaign messages, and safe layout behavior across each ratio.

task.title: Make every run reviewable
task.description: Produce reviewable outputs, run logs, and compliance checks so generated creative can be inspected before campaign use.

actions.title: Packaged a repeatable Python CLI
actions.description: Packaged the workflow as the pulse-cse Python command with structured brief loading, campaign logging, and repeatable output paths.

actions.title: Reused approved assets before generating
actions.description: Implemented local hero-asset reuse first, with optional GPT-5.5 image generation through the OpenAI Responses API and a visible placeholder fallback when live generation is unavailable.

actions.title: Composed three production ratios deterministically
actions.description: Used Pillow to crop and compose 1:1, 9:16, and 16:9 outputs with deterministic logo placement, localized text overlays, and brand color treatment.

actions.title: Logged provenance, compliance, and review output
actions.description: Recorded whether each hero was reused, generated, or replaced by fallback; checked dimensions, required logo application, campaign-message presence, and prohibited words; then emitted a browser review gallery and packageable review bundle.

results.title: Twelve outputs from one structured brief
results.description: The sample run produced four product-and-locale sets across two products, two locales, and three social ratios, for twelve final creative files.

results.title: Four localized sets passed documented checks
results.description: The run log confirmed expected dimensions, required logo application, campaign-message presence, and prohibited-word checks for each product-and-locale set.

results.title: A human review bundle remained the final gate
results.description: The workflow packages final creatives, the source brief, run log, compliance state, and review gallery for inspection. It demonstrates a tested local prototype rather than claiming autonomous campaign approval or production deployment.

material-explorer.title: Material Explorer
material-explorer.description: Material Explorer is a production-minded interactive web app for creating, comparing, and sharing physically based 3D materials in real time. Built with React + Three.js/React Three Fiber, it now includes autosave/restore, A/B compare, texture workflows, draft history, and shareable presets for rapid iteration.

details.proofRole: Direct shipped browser-product evidence for interactive 3D design tooling, spanning real-time PBR authoring, dual-canvas comparison, texture workflows, local-first persistence, share and GLB export, and automated quality gates.

situation.title: Material iteration was split across expert tools
situation.description: 3D material creation and customization typically requires specialized knowledge and software, creating barriers for designers and developers who want to experiment with materials for their web-based 3D projects. Existing tools often lack real-time feedback or are too complex for quick iterations.

situation.title: Feedback needed to be immediate
situation.description: The browser product needed to keep material values, lighting, model choice, and rendered appearance in one loop so authors could judge changes without switching tools or rebuilding a scene.

situation.title: Browser constraints shaped persistence and export
situation.description: The active beta stores materials locally by default, can fall back to local state when optional API sync fails, and must keep texture size, share-link length, and preview-geometry export limits visible.

task.title: Make PBR controls approachable
task.description: Expose physical material properties, textures, lighting, models, and preview controls in a product interface that designers and developers can operate directly.

task.title: Support rapid comparison and recovery
task.description: Give authors presets, autosave, draft history, undo and redo, A/B comparison, duplication, and randomization without interrupting the live preview.

task.title: Keep materials portable
task.description: Support library and single-material JSON, shareable material URLs, PNG snapshots, and GLB preview export while explaining which formats include textures or preview geometry.

actions.title: Built a live React Three Fiber lab
actions.description: Implemented real-time controls for color, metalness, roughness, emissive, clearcoat, transmission and IOR, opacity, seven texture-map channels, tiling, models, environments, and preview behavior.

actions.title: Added compare and iteration controls
actions.description: Shipped quick presets, draft autosave and restore, undo and redo history, dual-canvas A/B comparison, duplicate and randomize tools, section resets, and draft JSON transfer.

actions.title: Implemented local-first library workflows
actions.description: Added favorites, tags, search and sort, bulk actions, manual ordering, local persistence, optional API sync with local fallback, shareable URLs, JSON import and export, and GLB preview export.

actions.title: Hardened release quality
actions.description: Set up format, lint, type-check, Vitest, production build, bundle-budget, Playwright accessibility and smoke, and security-audit gates, with best-effort telemetry and a local mock sync backend for integration work.

results.title: One browser workflow from authoring to export
results.description: The public beta combines a live PBR editor, starter library, dual-canvas comparison, texture controls, draft recovery, sharing, and export in one inspectable browser product.

results.title: Local-first resilience with explicit limits
results.description: Materials remain usable through local storage when optional API sync is absent or unavailable. Embedded textures can exceed browser quotas, texture-rich share URLs can be rejected for size, and GLB output contains preview geometry rather than an imported user mesh.

results.title: A tested public beta, not a DCC replacement
results.description: The deployed GitHub Pages app and public repository demonstrate shipped React, TypeScript, and Three.js product engineering with automated quality gates. The project remains an active browser-material beta rather than claiming parity with production digital-content-creation suites.

vulnerability-visualizer.title: Vulnerability Visualizer
vulnerability-visualizer.description: Built a React + TypeScript security-review dashboard that keeps a 236,656-record vulnerability dataset searchable, visualizes manual-versus-AI assessment patterns, and exposes source provenance, record detail, comparison, and export controls.

details.proofRole: Direct shipped browser-product evidence for large-scale security review, spanning 236,656-record ingestion, virtualized exploration, explicit manual-versus-AI assessment modes, source provenance, record-level inspection, comparison controls, and filtered export.

situation.title: Security signal volume exceeded manual scanning
situation.description: The source data contains hundreds of thousands of vulnerability records with severity, package, status, score, dates, risk factors, manual assessment, and AI-assisted assessment fields. A flat table would leave reviewers searching without operational context.

situation.title: AI status needed to remain inspectable
situation.description: Manual and AI classifications can disagree. The review surface needed to expose those decision buckets and their effect on the working set instead of hiding the AI signal behind an opaque recommendation.

situation.title: Data availability could not block review
situation.description: The deployed app can load remote data, a prebuilt full dataset, a local sample, or mock records. Reviewers needed visible provenance so a fallback state could remain usable without being mistaken for the complete source.

task.title: Create a reviewable operating dashboard
task.description: Turn raw vulnerability records into a coherent workflow for searching, filtering, assessing severity, inspecting risk factors, opening record detail, comparing candidates, and exporting the current view.

task.title: Compare manual and AI classifications
task.description: Make the manual-analysis and AI-analysis modes explicit, quantify excluded records, and visualize where the two classification systems align or diverge.

task.title: Keep the full dataset operable
task.description: Preserve responsive filtering and navigation across the 236,656-record prebuilt runtime while retaining a clearly labeled fallback path when the full source is unavailable.

actions.title: Built normalized review controls
actions.description: Implemented debounced search, severity and status filters, sortable columns, All, Analysis, and AI Analysis modes, filter-impact counts, persisted preferences, and JSON or CSV export of the active result set.

actions.title: Visualized manual and AI decision buckets
actions.description: Added severity, analysis-status, risk-factor, trend, and manual-versus-AI relationship charts so reviewers can inspect both aggregate risk and classification disagreement.

actions.title: Connected overview to record-level evidence
actions.description: Linked critical highlights and virtualized table rows to a detail view with package, version, CVSS, status, risk factors, dates, references, and comparison controls.

actions.title: Hardened large-data loading
actions.description: Used React Query, react-window virtualization, lazy-loaded routes, source normalization, and a remote-to-prebuilt-to-local-sample-to-mock fallback chain while keeping the active data source visible in the interface.

results.title: 236,656 records in the prebuilt review surface
results.description: The deployed dashboard loaded the full prebuilt dataset with 236,656 vulnerabilities while preserving filters, charts, sortable and virtualized exploration, record detail, and export controls.

results.title: Manual-versus-AI review remained visible
results.description: The interface exposes separate manual and AI analysis modes, absolute filter-impact counts, and relationship charts so the AI-provided status is reviewable rather than presented as an unexplained verdict.

results.title: Resilient fallback with explicit provenance
results.description: When the full source was not ready, the deployed app remained usable with a ten-record local sample and labeled that source directly. The application does not conceal a fallback as full-dataset evidence.

results.title: A reviewer aid, not autonomous adjudication
results.description: The product visualizes and filters supplied manual and AI assessment fields; it does not independently determine vulnerability validity, remediate packages, or replace security judgment.

sound-escape-vr.title: Sound Escape VR
sound-escape-vr.description: Built a Unity VR music system that turns a four-channel, 16-step sequencer into an audio-reactive spatial performance, with persisted patterns, microphone sampling, eight-band FFT analysis, and Quest 3 interaction updates.

details.proofRole: Direct implemented Unity evidence for spatial music tooling, spanning a four-channel 16-step sequencer, persisted grid and tempo state, microphone sample capture, eight-band FFT analysis, audio-reactive Koch geometry, and Quest 3 input adaptation.

situation.title: Music tools and VR spectacle rarely shared one loop
situation.description: Traditional sequencers make composition precise but screen-bound, while many music experiences in VR remain passive visualizers. Sound Escape explored whether arranging a loop, hearing it, and watching the world respond could become one embodied interaction.

situation.title: Audio response needed to reflect the signal
situation.description: The environment could not rely on decorative animation alone. Frequency ranges, amplitude, timing, and user-selected samples needed to drive visible changes that remained connected to the music being composed.

situation.title: The Oculus-era prototype needed a hardware update
situation.description: The project began around an earlier Oculus interaction stack. Moving it to Unity 2022.3 and Meta Quest 3 required explicit work across device detection, floor tracking, controller input, grabbing, collision behavior, haptics, and Android XR configuration.

task.title: Make loop composition graspable in space
task.description: Create a direct sequencer surface where a player can place notes across four channels and 16 time slots, adjust tempo, preview the loop, and retain the pattern between sessions.

task.title: Connect sound structure to the environment
task.description: Translate live stereo spectrum data into stable frequency bands that could control Koch lines, trails, light, color, scale, and other spatial feedback.

task.title: Preserve player-created state
task.description: Keep grid patterns, tempo, sample origins, and short microphone recordings available locally so composition behaved like a tool rather than a one-pass visual scene.

task.title: Adapt the interaction layer for Quest 3
task.description: Update the XR camera, input bridge, controller profile, floor origin, grab behavior, hand collision, and haptic settings without presenting planned passthrough or voice work as finished functionality.

actions.title: Built the stateful 16-step sequencer
actions.description: Implemented a four-channel by 16-slot grid, scheduled sample playback against Unity DSP time, tempo control from 60 to 240 BPM, play and rewind state, and PlayerPrefs persistence for patterns and tempo.

actions.title: Added sample capture and local recovery
actions.description: Created four AudioSource channels with 80s synthwave defaults, six-second microphone recording, WAV persistence, sample-origin tracking, and local restoration of recorded or default clips.

actions.title: Mapped eight FFT bands into generative geometry
actions.description: Used stereo GetSpectrumData calls with a Blackman FFT window, normalized eight frequency bands and buffered amplitude, then drove Koch-line emission, trail color and width, lighting, and object scale from those values.

actions.title: Reworked input and tracking for Quest 3
actions.description: Added Quest-aware device detection, XRInput or OVRInput selection, floor-origin setup, controller deadzones, grip-or-trigger grabbing, raycast remote grab, hand-collision safeguards, haptic settings, and scene diagnostics for particle and light load.

results.title: A working composition-to-visualization loop
results.description: The repository contains the functional core sequencer, local pattern and tempo recovery, sample playback and recording paths, FFT analysis, and audio-reactive Koch geometry shown in the project documentation.

results.title: The world responds to authored audio state
results.description: Player-created patterns and samples feed the same eight-band signal system used by the neon geometry, connecting composition choices to emission, color, trail behavior, light, and scale.

results.title: Quest 3 compatibility became explicit code
results.description: A dedicated compatibility update added Meta Quest 3 detection and configuration across camera tracking, input, grab ergonomics, collisions, haptics, Android XR settings, and performance diagnostics.

results.title: A private-build prototype with a bounded roadmap
results.description: The public repository and demo document an implemented spatial music system, not a released consumer product. Exploration puzzles, a larger narrative journey, voice controls, broader accessibility work, multiplayer jams, and expanded fractal libraries remain planned or in refinement.

portals.title: Portals
portals.description: Built an accessible Snap Spectacles AR prototype that uses spatial audio and computer vision to connect music, culture, collaboration, and climate awareness.

details.client: Stanford's Immerse the Bay 2024 XR Hackathon

situation.title: Overview
situation.description: As AR technologies become more widespread, there's a growing need for experiences that prioritize accessibility, cultural education, and climate awareness. Many existing AR applications focus primarily on visual elements, often overlooking audio-based interactions and accessibility features.

situation.title: Challenge
situation.description: How might we create an immersive AR experience that makes diverse musical traditions accessible to everyone, including individuals with limited mobility, while promoting cultural education and climate awareness?

situation.title: Context
situation.description: Snap Spectacles provide a unique platform for AR development with spatial audio capabilities, offering new possibilities for creating inclusive and educational experiences.

task.title: Overview
task.description: The goal was to develop an AR experience for Snap Spectacles that would achieve four main objectives:

task.title: Accessibility
task.description: Design inclusive, voice-controlled features and audio cues for individuals with limited hand mobility or speech difficulties.

task.title: Cultural Education
task.description: Enable auditory exploration of music from various cultures, focusing on historical context and lesser-known instruments.

task.title: Joy
task.description: Create fun, collaborative AR jam sessions with friends using spatial audio.

task.title: Climate Change Awareness
task.description: Highlight music that connects to natural environments or climate themes to increase awareness through audio immersion.

actions.title: Co-Creation Features
actions.description: Developed spatial audio circles that position friends and collaborators in a virtual jam session, each playing an instrument or adding to the ambiance, allowing friends to interact audibly to avoid visual clutter.

actions.title: Interactive Auditory Timeline
actions.description: Implemented cultural markers recognition using Spectacles' computer vision to identify cultural artifacts or historical sites and launch a timeline with music, stories, and significant figures from that culture.

actions.title: Accessibility Implementation
actions.description: Created voice command controls for the experience, providing accessibility for individuals with limited mobility, along with a custom 'safe word' exit feature to prioritize user comfort and safety.

actions.title: Educational Layers
actions.description: Integrated optional deep dives into the history and significance of various music elements for educational purposes.

results.title: Inclusive AR Experience
results.description: Successfully created an AR experience that prioritizes accessibility through voice controls and audio-based interactions.

results.title: Cultural Education Platform
results.description: Developed a platform for exploring diverse musical traditions and their historical contexts, focusing on underrepresented communities.

results.title: Collaborative Music Creation
results.description: Implemented spatial audio features that enable users to create collaborative, immersive musical experiences with friends.

results.title: Climate Awareness Integration
results.description: Incorporated elements that highlight the connection between music, natural environments, and climate themes to raise awareness through immersive audio experiences.

die-ai.title: Die, AI!
die-ai.description: A Flash shooter demo built in Adobe Animate and ActionScript, now revived for modern browsers with Ruffle.

details.situation: Hackers reprogrammed AICorp's Buddy Bots to break out and destroy humanity. Dr. Gimmstein must stop them with his prototype laser gun.
details.task: Create a simple Flash game inspired by a shoot-em-up template and enhance it with levels, intro screens, and audio.

actions.title: Custom ActionScript Mechanics
actions.description: Extended a Shoot game template with health bars, lasers, enemy AI, and Greensock transitions.

actions.title: Ruffle Revival
actions.description: Repackaged the original SWF with an HTML loader using the Ruffle WebAssembly emulator.

details.result: A playable Flash demo preserved for the modern web.

ai-energy-consumption.title: AI Energy Consumption
ai-energy-consumption.description: Built an interactive 3D data visualization that explains AI energy consumption and CO2 emissions across countries and regions using A-Frame, D3.js, and clear data storytelling.

situation.title: Overview
situation.description: As AI technologies continue to advance and become more widespread, their energy consumption and environmental impact are growing concerns that often go unnoticed by the general public and even many technology professionals.

situation.title: Challenge
situation.description: Presenting complex environmental data in an engaging, accessible way is difficult. Traditional charts and graphs often fail to convey the scale and significance of the issue in a way that resonates with viewers.

situation.title: Context
situation.description: The rise of AI models with billions of parameters has led to exponential growth in computing requirements, with corresponding increases in energy consumption and carbon emissions.

task.title: Overview
task.description: The goal was to create an interactive 3D visualization that makes abstract data about AI energy consumption tangible and impactful for users.

task.title: Educational Objective
task.description: Raise awareness about the environmental impact of AI technologies in an engaging, interactive format.

task.title: Technical Objective
task.description: Combine web-based 3D visualization with accurate data representation to create an immersive learning experience.

actions.title: Research & Data Collection
actions.description: Gathered and analyzed data on AI energy consumption across different countries and regions, focusing on both training and inference phases of AI systems.

actions.title: Technical Implementation
actions.description: Developed a web-based 3D visualization using A-Frame for the immersive environment and D3.js for data binding and manipulation. Created custom shaders and animations to represent energy consumption patterns.

actions.title: User Experience Design
actions.description: Designed an intuitive interface that allows users to explore the data from different perspectives, with interactive elements that reveal additional information and context.

results.title: Engaging Visualization
results.description: Created a compelling 3D experience that transforms abstract data into a visually striking representation of AI's environmental impact.

results.title: Educational Tool
results.description: The project serves as both a data visualization and an educational resource, helping users understand the environmental implications of AI advancement.

results.title: Technical Achievement
results.description: Successfully integrated complex 3D graphics with data visualization techniques in a web-based application that performs well across different devices.
